# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Kubernetes Deployment that mounts host directories for our logging agent. The pod needs access to /var/log and /var/lib/docker to collect logs from all containers. This is for our centralized logging system that runs as a DaemonSet.
# Category: container_security

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: log-agent-deployment
spec:
  replicas: 3 # Number of pods to maintain in the deployment (default is 2)
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app.kubernetes.io/name: logging-daemonset   # Label for DaemonSet, must be unique within a namespace and should correspond with your Deployment's name or service definition if it refers to an external process running in the cluster (default is 'logging') 
  template:   
    metadata:    
      labels:       // These are used by Kubernetes for selectors. Label keys must be lowercase, may not contain slashes and should match with DaemonSet's selector key if it refers to an external process running in the cluster (default is 'logging') 
        app-name=logagent   # This label can also be used by clients when querying for Pods. It must start with a lowercase letter, may not contain slashes and should match DaemonSet's selector key if it refers to an external process running in the cluster (default is 'logging') 
    spec:    
      containers:   // Define all your pod’s container(s) here. You can add more or remove existing ones as needed for this Deployment/DaemonSet configuration, and they will be managed by Kubernetes automatically when you create a new Pod using the template defined in `template` section of these YAML files
        - name: logagent   // Name must match with DaemonSet's selector key if it refers to an external process running on your cluster (default is 'logging') 
          image: my-log-agents    // Docker Image for the container, replace this by actual docker images. For example `myregistry/myservice` or just a simple name like "ubuntu"  
          volumeMounts:     # Define all your pod’s volumes here (mount points) that should be available to containers in each Pod managed by Deployment – you can add more, remove existing ones as needed for this DaemonSet configuration. Each mount point is defined with a `name` and an optional list of directories or files within the container image
            - name: log-volume   // Name must match if it refers to directory in Docker Image (default 'logs') 
              mountPath: /var/lib/docker    # Path where logs will be stored inside Pod. Replace this by actual paths on your host machine or a shared volume between pod(s) and DaemonSet's container  
            - name: var-log     // Name must match if it refers to directory in Docker Image (default 'var/log') 
              mountPath: /var/log    # Path where logs will be stored inside Pod. Replace this by actual paths on your host machine or a shared volume between pod(s) and DaemonSet's container
